steps.title: Identify the rejected claim
steps.content: The underlined portion presents a tempting interpretation: Hair's fast-painting approach explains the Highwaymen aesthetic. It then rejects that interpretation as inaccurate because Hair's methods were not universally practiced by other Highwaymen. Harold Newton's example matters because he achieved the same effects through more deliberate painting.

steps.title: Avoid misread functions
steps.content: A wrongly focuses on one artist developing a style, C invents an aesthetic contrast between central and peripheral artists, and D invents a long-standing disagreement. The underlined portion raises and rebuts an interpretation about fast painting's aesthetic effect in the passage. The answer is B.
